title: Leg swelling differential documentation
source_review_basis: full authoritative documents inspected and archived; item-level retain/rewrite/remove/add comparisons recorded

assessment.text: Supports documenting swollen or painful leg context, general medical history, physical examination, laterality and swelling or tenderness findings, immobility, surgery, malignancy, previous DVT, and a clinician-entered score only. No score is calculated.
assessment.rationale: NG158 directly supports clinician documentation of a swollen or painful leg history and physical examination, including conditional thromboembolic risk features, while leaving the broad differential and any score entirely clinician-entered.

source.title: Venous thromboembolic diseases: diagnosis, management and thrombophilia testing
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
source.population: People presenting with signs or symptoms of deep vein thrombosis or pulmonary embolism and people receiving related follow-up.
source.setting: Initial medical assessment and clinician-led diagnostic pathways for suspected venous thromboembolic disease.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting a swollen or painful leg history, physical examination, conditional DVT or PE risk features, and clinician-entered assessment context. It does not calculate Wells or PERC scores, infer DVT or PE, order tests, prescribe anticoagulation, or determine urgency.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.1–1.1.2 and Table 1 — DVT history, examination, and clinician-scored features

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting swollen or painful leg context, general medical history, physical examination, laterality and swelling or tenderness findings, immobility, surgery, malignancy, previous DVT, and a clinician-entered score only. No score is calculated.

assessment.text: Supports documenting chest pain, shortness of breath, haemoptysis, vital-sign and examination context, prior VTE, immobilisation or surgery, malignancy, and a clinician-entered score only. No diagnosis or score is inferred.
assessment.rationale: The PE section supports documenting associated chest pain, breathlessness, haemoptysis, vital signs, examination, and clinician concern only when assessed; it does not infer PE or urgency.

source.title: Venous thromboembolic diseases: diagnosis, management and thrombophilia testing
source.jurisdiction: England and Wales; international evidence requiring UAE adaptation
source.population: People presenting with signs or symptoms of deep vein thrombosis or pulmonary embolism and people receiving related follow-up.
source.setting: Initial medical assessment and clinician-led diagnostic pathways for suspected venous thromboembolic disease.
source.exclusions: Exact for documenting a swollen or painful leg history, physical examination, conditional DVT or PE risk features, and clinician-entered assessment context. It does not calculate Wells or PERC scores, infer DVT or PE, order tests, prescribe anticoagulation, or determine urgency.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.1.15–1.1.17 and Table 2 — PE history, examination, and clinician-scored features

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports documenting chest pain, shortness of breath, haemoptysis, vital-sign and examination context, prior VTE, immobilisation or surgery, malignancy, and a clinician-entered score only. No diagnosis or score is inferred.

assessment.text: Supports clinical history and examination, NT-proBNP, echocardiography, ECG, chest X-ray, renal, thyroid, liver, lipid, glucose, blood count, and urinalysis review.
assessment.rationale: NG106 supports documenting edema with cardiorespiratory history, examination, and already reviewed cardiac or laboratory results when heart failure is clinically considered, without concluding a cardiac cause.

source.title: Chronic heart failure in adults: diagnosis and management
source.population: Adults with suspected or established chronic heart failure.
source.setting: Primary, community, and specialist cardiovascular care.
source.exclusions: Exact for adult chronic-heart-failure diagnosis, review, monitoring, and clinician-led management; international only and not directly validated for UAE pathways.

exact_location.heading: Recommendations 1.2.1–1.2.12 — history, examination, natriuretic peptides, imaging, ECG, and laboratory tests

source.evidence_paraphrase: Supports clinical history and examination, NT-proBNP, echocardiography, ECG, chest X-ray, renal, thyroid, liver, lipid, glucose, blood count, and urinalysis review.

assessment.text: Supports documenting an already agreed monitoring or follow-up plan and information already provided for confirmed or suspected low-risk PE; it does not generate that plan.
assessment.rationale: Follow-up guidance was reviewed but not mapped because the generic swelling workflow does not establish VTE or low-risk outpatient status.
